did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper used deep learning, specifically a hybrid model integrating EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121 convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention mechanisms, to perform multi-class classification of skin lesions from the ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents original experimental work developing a hybrid deep learning model (EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121 with Squeeze-and-Excitation attention) for skin lesion classification. It details the methodology, training on ISIC 2019/2020 datasets, and reports specific experimental results (e.g., 96.04% accuracy). Although published in a conference proceedings (DASA), it contains original research results rather than summarizing prior literature.

title: Classification of skin lesions using squeeze and excitation attention based hybrid model of DenseNet and EfficientNet
journal: 2024 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
brief_summary: This study developed a hybrid deep learning model for the multi-class classification of eight skin lesion categories to improve early skin cancer diagnosis. The proposed architecture integrates EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121 convolutional neural networks with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention mechanisms to enhance feature extraction. The models were trained and evaluated using combined ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets. Experimental results demonstrated that the EfficientNetV2-B0 model with SE attention achieved the highest accuracy of 96.04% and an AUC of 96.12%. The integration of the SE attention mechanism significantly improved classification performance compared to standalone CNN models, reducing misdiagnosis risks.

lesion_types_studied.details: One of the eight skin lesion categories classified in the study using ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets. ISIC-2020 'nevus images' were renamed to NV.
One of the eight skin lesion categories classified. Identified as having a higher likelihood of growth and spread compared to other types of skin cancer.

all_task_types.target: Eight discrete skin lesion categories (Nevus, Melanoma, Benign Keratosis Lesion, Dermatofibroma, Actinic Keratosis, Vascular Lesion, Basal Cell Carcinoma, Squamous Cell Carcinoma)
all_task_types.details: Multi-class classification of skin lesions using a hybrid deep learning model (EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121 with Squeeze-and-Excitation attention) trained on ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets. The model predicts discrete diagnostic categories rather than continuous values. Performance was evaluated using accuracy (96.04% for EfficientNetV2-B0 + SE) and AUC (96.12%).

datasets_used.name: ISIC 2019, 2020
datasets_used.description: Public dermoscopic image datasets from the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC). The study combined the 2019 and 2020 datasets, renaming classes for consistency (e.g., seborrheic keratosis, lichenoid keratosis, solar lentigo, and lentigo NOS to BKL; nevus to NV; melanoma to MEL; cafe-au-lait macule and atypical melanocytic proliferation to unknown). The unknown class was removed, resulting in a final dataset of 31,331 images.

use.training: Used for training the hybrid EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121 models with data augmentation (rotation, flips, zoom, shear). 70% of the data was used for training.
use.validation: Used for validation during the training process. 15% of the data was used for validation.
use.testing: Used for final evaluation of model performance (accuracy, AUC, confusion matrix). 15% of the data was used for testing.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report any formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance improvements were described using absolute percentage point increases in accuracy and AUC metrics without statistical significance testing.

ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 (Combined).notes: The EfficientNetV2-B0 + SE model achieved the highest overall performance. The SE attention mechanism improved DenseNet121 AUC by 2.45% and accuracy by 1.09%. It improved EfficientNetV2-B0 AUC by 0.31% and accuracy by 0.74%. The EfficientNetV2-B0 + SE model correctly predicted 68 more lesions than the standalone EfficientNetV2-B0, while DenseNet121 + SE correctly predicted 268 more than standalone DenseNet121.

Per-Class AUC Results.notes: NV: Nevus, MEL: Melanoma, BKL: Benign Keratosis Lesion, DF: Dermatofibroma, SCC: Squamous Cell Carcinoma, BCC: Basal Cell Carcinoma, VASC: Vascular Lesion, AK: Actinic Keratosis.

Per-Class Accuracy Results.notes: NV: Nevus, MEL: Melanoma, BKL: Benign Keratosis Lesion, DF: Dermatofibroma, SCC: Squamous Cell Carcinoma, BCC: Basal Cell Carcinoma, VASC: Vascular Lesion, AK: Actinic Keratosis.

architecture_used.main_model: Hybrid model integrating EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121 with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention mechanisms
architecture_used.pretraining: Pre-trained models were used (specifically EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121), though specific pretraining datasets (e.g., ImageNet) are not explicitly stated in the provided text, the term 'pre-trained' is used in Section III.D.

architecture_details.attention_mechanism: Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention mechanism added to the final layers of the CNN models
se_block_stages: Squeeze: Extracts global statistics from feature maps via average or max pooling
Excitation: Computes importance scores for each channel using fully connected layers

preprocessing_techniques: Dataset combination: Combined ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets.

Class renaming and filtering: Renamed classes for consistency (e.g., seborrheic keratosis, lichenoid keratosis, solar lentigo, and lentigo NOS to BKL; nevus to NV; melanoma to MEL; cafe-au-lait macule and atypical melanocytic proliferation to unknown). Removed the 'unknown' class.
Data splitting: Divided the final dataset of 31,331 images into 70% training, 15% validation, and 15% testing.
Rescaling: Applied rescaling factor of 1./255 to training data.
Data augmentation (training only): Applied rotation range of 20%, horizontal flip, vertical flip, zoom range of 0.2, shear range of 0.2, and fill mode 'nearest'.
Validation preprocessing: Applied only normalization (rescaling) to validation data; no augmentation was applied to validation or test data.

data_augmentation_techniques.details: Applied to training data with a factor of 1./255.
Applied with a rotation range of 20%.
Applied with a zoom range of 0.2.
Applied with a shear range of 0.2.

conclusions: The integration of the Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention mechanism into hybrid CNN architectures (EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121) significantly improves multi-class skin lesion classification performance compared to standalone CNN models.
The EfficientNetV2-B0 model with SE attention achieved the highest performance, with an accuracy of 96.04% and an AUC of 96.12%.
The SE attention mechanism improved the AUC of DenseNet121 by 2.45% (from 92.16% to 94.61%) and its accuracy by 1.09% (from 93.88% to 94.97%).
The SE attention mechanism improved the AUC of EfficientNetV2-B0 by 0.31% (from 95.81% to 96.12%) and its accuracy by 0.74% (from 95.30% to 96.04%).

The EfficientNetV2-B0 + SE model correctly predicted 68 more skin lesions than the standalone EfficientNetV2-B0, while the DenseNet121 + SE model correctly predicted 268 more lesions than the standalone DenseNet121.
The proposed hybrid model demonstrates robustness in handling imbalanced data without requiring dataset size alteration or extensive balancing techniques.
The study contributes to reducing misdiagnosis risks and improving the speed and accuracy of the initial visual diagnosis process for skin cancer.
The developed model offers potential for cost savings for hospitals and clinics with limited resources by enabling faster and more accurate early detection.
important_remarks: The study relies exclusively on the combined ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets, which may limit generalizability to other populations or clinical settings due to potential dataset bias.
No external validation was performed; the model was only tested on the held-out 15% split of the combined ISIC datasets.
The paper does not report any statistical significance testing (e.g., p-values or confidence intervals) for the reported performance improvements, relying solely on absolute metric differences.
The 'unknown' class from the ISIC 2020 dataset was removed during preprocessing, which may affect the model's ability to handle ambiguous cases in real-world clinical scenarios.
Data leakage risks are present as the ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets were combined without explicit mention of ensuring patient-level separation between training and testing sets.

The study claims robustness against imbalanced data but does not report specific class distribution statistics or employ explicit balancing techniques (e.g., SMOTE, class weights), relying instead on the SE attention mechanism.
The model was trained for only 50 epochs, which may be insufficient to fully converge complex hybrid architectures compared to standard training durations in similar literature.

The paper does not provide a detailed breakdown of computational resources (e.g., FLOPs, inference time) despite claiming efficiency, limiting the assessment of deployment feasibility in resource-constrained environments.

dataset_usage_details.summary: The study utilized a combined dataset derived from the ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 archives. The datasets were merged, classes were renamed for consistency (e.g., seborrheic keratosis to BKL), and the 'unknown' class was removed, resulting in a final pool of 31,331 images across eight classes. This combined dataset was split into 70% for training, 15% for validation, and 15% for testing. No external or cross-dataset validation was performed; all experiments were conducted on this single pooled dataset.
dataset_usage_details.cross_dataset_validation_details: The paper did not perform cross-dataset validation. The ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets were pooled together into a single dataset before splitting. There is no evidence of training on one source and testing on a separate held-out source from the other.

training.notes: Used for model fitting with data augmentation (rotation, flips, zoom, shear) and rescaling. The 'unknown' class was removed prior to training.

validation.notes: Used for model selection/tuning during the training process. Only normalization (rescaling) was applied; no augmentation.

testing.notes: Used for final performance evaluation (accuracy, AUC, confusion matrix). No augmentation was applied.

pretraining_or_transfer_learning.notes: The EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121 models are standard architectures typically pretrained on ImageNet. The paper states they are 'pre-trained' but does not explicitly name ImageNet as the source, though it is the standard default for these architectures in this context.

synthetic_or_augmented_data.notes: Data augmentation was applied to the training set only: rotation (20%), horizontal flip, vertical flip, zoom (0.2), shear (0.2), and fill mode 'nearest'. No synthetic data generation was reported.

evaluation_protocol.split_details: 70% training, 15% validation, 15% testing. Total 31,331 images. Patient-level or image-level separation is not explicitly reported, raising potential leakage risks given the pooling of ISIC 2019 and 2020.
evaluation_protocol.unseen_data_details: No unseen external dataset was used. The test set was a held-out 15% split from the combined ISIC 2019/2020 pool.

dataset_level_results.notes: Best performing model. Correctly predicted 68 more lesions than standalone EfficientNetV2-B0.
Correctly predicted 268 more lesions than standalone DenseNet121.

generalizability_notes: The study relies exclusively on the combined ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets, which limits generalizability to other populations or clinical settings due to potential dataset bias.
No external validation was performed; the model was only tested on the held-out 15% split of the combined ISIC datasets.
Data leakage risks are present as the ISIC 2019 and ISIC 2020 datasets were combined without explicit mention of ensuring patient-level separation between training and testing sets.
The 'unknown' class from the ISIC 2020 dataset was removed, which may affect the model's ability to handle ambiguous cases in real-world clinical scenarios.
information_not_reported: Exact patient-level separation between training and testing sets is not reported.
Specific class distribution statistics (counts per class) for the final 31,331 image dataset are not reported.
Explicit confirmation that ImageNet was the pretraining source for EfficientNetV2-B0 and DenseNet121, though implied by architecture names.
Confidence intervals or p-values for the reported performance metrics.
